name: Lakera Guard API
what_it_does: Real-time AI security layer that detects and blocks prompt injection attacks, jailbreak attempts, and harmful LLM outputs with sub-50ms latency.
use_cases: Gate all untrusted user inputs before they reach an LLM in an agent pipeline
Scan LLM outputs for harmful content, PII, or policy violations before delivery
Protect multi-agent systems from indirect prompt injection via retrieved content
Enforce content policies in customer-facing AI products without adding significant latency
Audit and log AI interaction safety signals for compliance and incident review
not_for: General DLP or data classification beyond AI interaction content
Replacing comprehensive content moderation systems for non-AI workloads
best_when: Best when building agent pipelines that process untrusted user input or retrieved external content before passing it to an LLM — inline safety gating with minimal latency impact.
avoid_when: Avoid when your pipeline has no untrusted input surface, or when the $499/month starting price is prohibitive and open-source alternatives like Rebuff are acceptable.

auth.notes: Single API key per project passed as Bearer token. Key management available via platform dashboard. No scope granularity currently.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 1,000 API calls per day on free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Paid plans from $499/month; enterprise pricing for high-volume or on-premises deployments
pricing.notes: Free tier is generous enough for development and testing. Production agent workloads will typically require a paid plan.

performance.rate_limits: Free tier: 1,000 calls/day; paid plans offer higher limits defined per contract

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns structured detection results with category labels and confidence scores; clear distinction between safe, flagged, and blocked outcomes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Detection requests are stateless and fully idempotent — safe to retry on transient errors.
known_agent_gotchas: OpenAI-compatible proxy wrapper is convenient but hides raw detection signals from agent logic
Free tier daily reset time is UTC midnight — agents with 24h workflows may hit limits unexpectedly
Detection categories evolve over time — pin to specific policy versions in production to avoid behavior drift
MCP integration is newer and less battle-tested than the REST API

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 certified. Input data is not retained for training without explicit consent. TLS enforced on all endpoints.
